t.khattab: Is it not clear to those who take over the land after ˹the destruction of˺ its former residents that—if We will—We can punish them ˹too˺ for their sins and seal their hearts so they will not hear ˹the truth˺
t.ghali: And is it not a guidance (Literally: Does it not guide them) to the ones who inherit the earth after its population that, if We (so) decide, We will afflict them for their guilty deeds, and We stamp upon their hearts so they do not hear
t.irving: Were those who will inherit the earth after its (present] people never shown how We would strike them down for their offences and seal off their hearts if We so wished, so they would not hear
t.iwq: Has it not become clear to those who inherited the earth after its previous people that if We willed, We could afflict them for their sins? But We seal over their hearts so they do not hear

t_id.kemenag: Dan apakah belum jelas bagi orang-orang yang mempusakai suatu negeri sesudah (lenyap) penduduknya, bahwa kalau Kami menghendaki tentu Kami azab mereka karena dosa-dosanya; dan Kami kunci mati hati mereka sehingga mereka tidak dapat mendengar (pelajaran lagi)?
t_id.sabiq: Apakah orang-orang yang meneruskan bangsa-bangsa terdahulu lupa akan ketentuan Allah yang berlaku untuk mereka, bahwa sikap Kami terhadap mereka sama seperti terhadap para pendahulu mereka? Yaitu, mereka semua tunduk kepada kehendak Kami. Jika Kami berkehendak menyiksa mereka karena dosa, maka niscaya kami siksa mereka seperti yang lainnya. Kami kunci hati mereka karena kelewat rusak sehingga tidak bisa lagi menerima petunjuk. Dengan kunci dan penutup tersebut mereka tidak bisa mendengarkan nasihat untuk dipahami dan dipetik pelajarannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan apakah belum jelas) artinya belum terang (bagi orang-orang yang mempusakai bumi ini) sebagai tempat tinggalnya (sesudah) binasanya (penduduknya bahwa) menjadi fa'il berasal dari anna yang ditakhfifkan sedangkan isimnya dibuang, artinya bahwasanya (kalau Kami menghendaki tentu Kami timpakan kepada mereka siksaan) yakni azab (karena dosa-dosanya) sebagaimana telah Kami timpakan siksaan kepada orang-orang sebelum mereka. Kesemua hamzah di empat tempat tersebut semuanya bermakna lit-taubikh/mencela; dan huruf fa dan wawu yang memasuki pada kedua di antaranya untuk tujuan athaf. Menurut suatu qiraat dibaca dengan wawu yang disukunkan pada tempat yang pertama karena diathafkan kepada huruf aw. (Dan) Kami (kunci) Kami lak (hati mereka sehingga mereka tidak dapat mendengar) nasihat dengan pendengaran yang sehat.
t_id.iwq: Dan apakah belum jelas bagi orang-orang yang mempusakai suatu negeri sesudah (lenyap) penduduknya, bahwa kalau Kami menghendaki tentu Kami azab mereka karena dosa-dosanya; dan Kami kunci mati hati mereka sehingga mereka tidak dapat mendengar (pelajaran lagi)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أو لم يهد» يتبيَّن «للذين يرثون الأرض» بالسكنى «من بعد» هلاك «أهلها أن» فاعل مخففة واسمها محذوف أي أنه «لو نشاء أصبناهم» بالعذاب «بذنوبهم» كما أصبنا من قبلهم. والهمزةُ في المواضع الأربعة للتوبيخ والفاء والواو الداخلة عليهما للعطف، وفي قراءة بسكون الواو في الموضع الأول عطفا بأو «و» نحن «نطبع» نختم «على قلوبهم فهم لا يسمعون» الموعظة سماع تدبر
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَوَلَمۡ يَهۡدِ لِلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ أَهۡلِهَآ أَن لَّوۡ نَشَآءُ أَصَبۡنَٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمۡۚ وَنَطۡبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمۡ فَهُمۡ لَا يَسۡمَعُونَ (أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ: أَوَلَمْ يَتَبَيَّنْ, يَرِثُونَ: يَسْكُنُونَ, وَنَطْبَعُ: نَخْتِمُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan apakah belum jelas) artinya belum terang (bagi orang-orang yang mempusakai bumi ini) sebagai tempat tinggalnya (sesudah) binasanya (penduduknya bahwa) menjadi fa'il berasal dari anna yang ditakhfifkan sedangkan isimnya dibuang, artinya bahwasanya (kalau Kami menghendaki tentu Kami timpakan kepada mereka siksaan) yakni azab (karena dosa-dosanya) sebagaimana telah Kami timpakan siksaan kepada orang-orang sebelum mereka. Kesemua hamzah di empat tempat tersebut semuanya bermakna lit-taubikh/mencela; dan huruf fa dan wawu yang memasuki pada kedua di antaranya untuk tujuan athaf. Menurut suatu qiraat dibaca dengan wawu yang disukunkan pada tempat yang pertama karena diathafkan kepada huruf aw. (Dan) Kami (kunci) Kami lak (hati mereka sehingga mereka tidak dapat mendengar) nasihat dengan pendengaran yang sehat

ar: تِلۡكَ ٱلۡقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّ عَلَیۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَاۤئِهَاۚ وَلَقَدۡ جَاۤءَتۡهُمۡ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلۡبَیِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِیُؤۡمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ مِن قَبۡلُۚ كَذَ ٰلِكَ یَطۡبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلۡكَـٰفِرِینَ
ar_simple: تلك ٱلقرى نقص علیك من أنبائها ولقد جاءتهم رسلهم بٱلبینت فما كانوا لیؤمنوا بما كذبوا من قبل كذ لك یطبع ٱلله على قلوب ٱلكفرین

t.sahih-int: Those cities - We relate to you, [O Muhammad], some of their news. And certainly did their messengers come to them with clear proofs, but they were not to believe in that which they had denied before. Thus does Allah seal over the hearts of the disbelievers
t.asad: Unto those [earlier] communities - some of whose stories We [now] relate unto thee -there had indeed come apostles of their own with all evidence of the truth; but they would not believe in anything to which they had once given the lie: thus it is that God seals the hearts of those who deny the truth
t.yusuf-ali: Such were the towns whose story We (thus) relate unto thee: There came indeed to them their messengers with clear (signs): But they would not believe what they had rejected before. Thus doth Allah seal up the hearts of those who reject faith
t.pickthall: Such were the townships. We relate some tidings of them unto thee (Muhammad). Their messengers verily came unto them with clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty), but they could not believe because they had before denied. Thus doth Allah print upon the hearts of disbelievers (that they hear not)
t.arberry: Those cities We relate to thee tidings of; their Messengers came to them with the clear signs, but they were not the ones to believe in that they had cried lies before; so God seals the hearts of the unbelievers
t.hilali-khan: Those were the towns whose story We relate unto you (O Muhammad SAW). And there came indeed to them their Messengers with clear proofs, but they were not such as to believe in that which they had rejected before. Thus Allah does seal up the hearts of the disbelievers (from each and every kind of religious guidance)
t.itani: These towns—We narrate to you some of their tales. Their messengers came to them with the clear signs, but they would not believe in what they had rejected previously. Thus God seals the hearts of the disbelievers.
t.mubarakpuri: Those were the towns whose story We relate unto you. And there came indeed to them their Messengers with clear proofs, but they were not such who would believe in what they rejected before. Thus Allah does seal up the hearts of the disbelievers.
t.qarib: To those [earlier] communities, some of whose stories We relate to you, messengers from among them came with evidence of the truth, but they would not believe in anything which they had already rejected. God seals the hearts of the unbelievers
t.wahiduddin: We have told you the stories of those towns: their messengers came to them with clear signs, but they were never going to believe in something they had already rejected. Thus God seals up the hearts of those who deny the truth
t.shakir: These towns-- We relate to you some of their stories, and certainly their apostles came to them with clear arguments, but they would not believe in what they rejected at first; thus does Allah set a seal over the hearts of the unbelievers
t.haleem: We have told you [Prophet] the stories of those towns: messengers came to them, and clear signs, but they would not believe in what they had already rejected- in this way God seals the hearts of disbelievers
t.maariful: Those are the towns We narrate to you their important events. Surely their messengers came to them with clear signs, but they were not able to believe in what they had rejected earlier. This is how Allah seals the hearts of the disbelievers
t.khattab: We have narrated to you ˹O Prophet˺ some of the stories of those societies. Surely, their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but still they would not believe in what they had already denied. This is how Allah seals the hearts of the disbelievers
t.ghali: Such were the towns We narrate (some) tidings of them to you, and indeed their Messengers already came to them with the supremely evident (signs). So in no way could they believe in what they had cried lies (to) earlier. Thus Allah stamps upon the hearts of the disbelievers
t.irving: We have related news concerning those towns to you: their messengers came to them with explanations, yet they were not in any mood to believe in something they had already rejected. Thus God seals off disbelievers´ hearts
t.iwq: Those cities - We relate to you, O Muhammad, some of their news. And certainly did their messengers come to them with clear proofs, but they were not to believe in that which they had denied before. Thus does Allah seal over the hearts of the disbelievers

t_id.kemenag: Negeri-negeri (yang telah Kami binasakan) itu, Kami ceritakan sebagian dari berita-beritanya kepadamu. Dan sungguh telah datang kepada mereka rasul-rasul mereka dengan membawa bukti-bukti yang nyata, maka mereka (juga) tidak beriman kepada apa yang dahulunya mereka telah mendustakannya. Demikianlah Allah mengunci mata hati orang-orang kafir.
t_id.sabiq: Negeri-negeri yang masa dan sejarahnya telah lampau jauh itu Kami ceritakan kembali saat ini sebagian beritanya yang mengandung pelajaran. Telah datang kepada penduduk negeri-negeri itu rasul- rasul dengan membawa bukti-bukti jelas yang menunjukkan kebenaran misi mereka. Mereka tidak beriman setelah datangnya bukti-bukti yang jelas itu, karena selalu mendustakan orang-orang yang benar. Mereka juga mendustakan para rasul dan tidak mendapat petunjuk. Demikianlah Allah membuat penghalang atas hati dan akal orang-orang kafir. Dengan begitu, jalan kebenaran menjadi tak tampak dan mereka jauh dari kebenaran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Negeri-negeri itu) yang telah disebutkan tadi (Kami ceritakan kepadamu) hai Muhammad (tentang sebagian dari berita-beritanya) cerita-cerita penduduknya (Dan sungguh telah datang kepada mereka rasul-rasul mereka dengan membawa bukti-bukti) yaitu mukjizat-mukjizat yang selalu unggul lagi jelas (maka mereka juga tidak beriman) tatkala rasul-rasul itu datang (kepada apa yang dahulu mereka telah mendustakannya) yang telah mereka ingkari (sebelum itu) sebelum para rasul itu datang, bahkan mereka tetap terus melakukan kekafirannya. (Demikianlah) seperti penguncian itu (Allah mengunci mati hati orang-orang kafir).
t_id.iwq: Negeri-negeri (yang telah Kami binasakan) itu, Kami ceritakan sebagian dari berita-beritanya kepadamu. Dan sungguh telah datang kepada mereka rasul-rasul mereka dengan membawa bukti-bukti yang nyata, maka mereka (juga) tidak beriman kepada apa yang dahulunya mereka telah mendustakannya. Demikianlah Allah mengunci mata hati orang-orang kafir.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «تلك القرى» التي مرَّ ذكرها «نقصُّ عليك» يا محمد «من أنبائها» أخبار أهلها «ولقد جاءتهم رسلهم بالبينات» المعجزات الظاهرات «فما كانوا ليؤمنوا» عند مجيئهم «بما كذبوا» كفروا به «من قبل» قبل مجيئهم بل استمروا على الكفر «كذلك» الطبع «يطبع الله على قلوب الكافرين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: تِلۡكَ ٱلۡقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّ عَلَيۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَآئِهَاۚ وَلَقَدۡ جَآءَتۡهُمۡ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ فَمَا كَانُواْ لِيُؤۡمِنُواْ بِمَا كَذَّبُواْ مِن قَبۡلُۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطۡبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Negeri-negeri itu) yang telah disebutkan tadi (Kami ceritakan kepadamu) hai Muhammad (tentang sebagian dari berita-beritanya) cerita-cerita penduduknya (Dan sungguh telah datang kepada mereka rasul-rasul mereka dengan membawa bukti-bukti) yaitu mukjizat-mukjizat yang selalu unggul lagi jelas (maka mereka juga tidak beriman) tatkala rasul-rasul itu datang (kepada apa yang dahulu mereka telah mendustakannya) yang telah mereka ingkari (sebelum itu) sebelum para rasul itu datang, bahkan mereka tetap terus melakukan kekafirannya. (Demikianlah) seperti penguncian itu (Allah mengunci mati hati orang-orang kafir)

ar: وَمَا وَجَدۡنَا لِأَكۡثَرِهِم مِّنۡ عَهۡدࣲۖ وَإِن وَجَدۡنَاۤ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ لَفَـٰسِقِینَ
ar_simple: وما وجدنا لأكثرهم من عهد وإن وجدنا أكثرهم لفسقین

t.sahih-int: And We did not find for most of them any covenant; but indeed, We found most of them defiantly disobedient
t.asad: and in most of them We found no [inner] bond with anything that is right -and most of them We found to be iniquitous indeed
t.yusuf-ali: Most of them We found not men (true) to their covenant: but most of them We found rebellious and disobedient
t.pickthall: We found no (loyalty to any) covenant in most of them. Nay, most of them We found wrong-doers
t.arberry: We found no covenant in the most part of them; indeed, We found the most part of them ungodly
t.hilali-khan: And most of them We found not (true) to their covenant, but most of them We found indeed Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah)
t.itani: We found most of them untrue to their covenants; We found most of them corrupt.
t.mubarakpuri: And most of them We found not true to their covenant, but most of them We found indeed rebellious.
t.qarib: and in most of them We found that they did not honor their commitments. We found that most of them were defiant
t.wahiduddin: We found most of them untrue to their covenants, indeed We found most of them to be defiant
t.shakir: And We did not find in most of them any (faithfulness to) covenant, and We found most of them to be certainly transgressors
t.haleem: We found that most of them did not honour their commitments; We found that most of them were defiant
t.maariful: We did not find with most of them any covenant (unbroken), and surely We have found most of them sinners
t.khattab: We did not find most of them true to their covenant. Rather, We found most of them truly rebellious
t.ghali: And in no way did We find (loyalty to any) covenant in most of them, and decidedly We found most of them immoral indeed
t.irving: We did not find any agreement was ever kept by most of them, although we did find most of them were quite immoral
t.iwq: And We did not find for most of them any covenant; but verily, We found most of them defiantly disobedient

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami tidak mendapati kebanyakan mereka memenuhi janji. Sesungguhnya Kami mendapati kebanyakan mereka orang-orang yang fasik.
t_id.sabiq: Kami tidak mendapati kebanyakan mereka itu memenuhi janji berupa keimanan yang Kami pesankan kepada mereka melalui para rasul dan nalar yang sehat. Tetapi keadaan kebanyakan mereka selalu dalam kefasikan dan melanggar perjanjian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami tidak mendapati kebanyakan mereka) maksudnya kebanyakan umat manusia itu (memenuhi janji) menunaikan janji mereka tatkala tiba saat pemenuhannya (Sesungguhnya) ditakhfifkan dari anna (Kami mendapati kebanyakan mereka orang-orang yang fasik).
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami tidak mendapati kebanyakan mereka memenuhi janji. Sesungguhnya Kami mendapati kebanyakan mereka orang-orang yang fasik.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما وجدنا لأكثرهم» أي الناس «من عهد» أي وفاء بعهدهم يوم أخذ الميثاق «وإن» مخففة «وجدنا أكثرهم لفاسقين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا وَجَدۡنَا لِأَكۡثَرِهِم مِّنۡ عَهۡدٖۖ وَإِن وَجَدۡنَآ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ لَفَٰسِقِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami tidak mendapati kebanyakan mereka) maksudnya kebanyakan umat manusia itu (memenuhi janji) menunaikan janji mereka tatkala tiba saat pemenuhannya (Sesungguhnya) ditakhfifkan dari anna (Kami mendapati kebanyakan mereka orang-orang yang fasik)

ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثۡنَا مِنۢ بَعۡدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ بِءَایَـٰتِنَاۤ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِی۟هِۦ فَظَلَمُوا۟ بِهَاۖ فَٱنظُرۡ كَیۡفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلۡمُفۡسِدِینَ
ar_simple: ثم بعثنا من بعدهم موسى بءایتنا إلى فرعون وملإیه فظلموا بها فٱنظر كیف كان عقبة ٱلمفسدین

t.sahih-int: Then We sent after them Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, but they were unjust toward them. So see how was the end of the corrupters
t.asad: AND AFTER those [early people] We sent Moses with Our messages unto Pharaoh and his great ones, and they wilfully rejected them: and behold what happened in the end to those spreaders of corruption
t.yusuf-ali: Then after them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they wrongfully rejected them: So see what was the end of those who made mischief
t.pickthall: Then, after them, We sent Moses with our tokens unto Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they repelled them. Now, see the nature of the consequence for the corrupters
t.arberry: Then We sent, after them, Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his Council, but they did them wrong; so behold thou, how was the end of the workers of corruption
t.hilali-khan: Then after them We sent Musa (Moses) with Our Signs to Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his chiefs, but they wrongfully rejected them. So see how was the end of the Mufsidun (mischief-makers, corrupts, etc)
t.itani: Then, after them, We sent Moses with Our miracles to Pharaoh and his establishment, but they denounced them. So consider the end of the evildoers.
t.mubarakpuri: Then after them We sent Musa with Our signs to Fir`awn and his chiefs, but they wrongfully rejected them. So see how was the end of the mischief-makers
t.qarib: Later, We sent Moses with Our messages to Pharaoh and his inner circle, and they rejected them. Consider what happened to those who used to spread corruption
t.wahiduddin: After them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs. But they willfully rejected them. Consider the end of the evil-doers
t.shakir: Then we raised after them Musa with Our communications to Firon and his chiefs, but they disbelieved in them; consider then what was the end of the mischief makers
t.haleem: After these, We sent Moses to Pharaoh and his leading supporters with Our signs, but they rejected them. See the fate of those who used to spread corruption
t.maariful: After them, We sent Mūsā with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs. But they did injustice to them. So, consider the fate of the mischief-makers
t.khattab: Then after them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, but they wrongfully rejected them. See what was the end of the corruptors
t.ghali: Thereafter We sent forth even after them Musa (Moses) with Our signs to Firaawn (Pharaoh) and his chiefs, yet they did injustice regarding them. So look how was the end of the corruptors
t.irving: Then after them, we dispatched Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his courtiers, and they mistreated them. See how the outcome was for mischiefmakers
t.iwq: Then We sent after them Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, but they were unjust toward them. Thus see how was the end of the corrupters

t_id.kemenag: Kemudian Kami utus Musa sesudah rasul-rasul itu dengan membawa ayat-ayat Kami kepada Fir'aun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya, lalu mereka mengingkari ayat-ayat itu. Maka perhatikanlah bagaimana akibat orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan.
t_id.sabiq: Setelah rasul-rasul itu, Kami mengutus Mûsâ a. s. kepada Fir'aun dan kaumnya, dengan membawa bukti-bukti yang menunjukkan kebenarannya dalam menyampaikan wahyu Kami. Kepada mereka, Mûsâ pun menyampaikan seruan Tuhannya. Diperlihatkannyalah kepada mereka ayat-ayat Allah. Tetapi, dengan sikap takabur, mereka menganiaya diri dengan mengingkari hal itu. Karena itulah mereka, akhirnya, berhak mendapat azab Allah. Maka cermatilah, wahai Nabi, akhir perjalanan orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami utus sesudah rasul-rasul itu) sesudah diutusnya rasul-rasul tersebut (Musa dengan membawa ayat-ayat Kami) yang banyaknya sembilan (kepada Firaun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya) golongannya (lalu mereka mengingkari) mengkafiri (ayat-ayat itu. Maka perhatikanlah bagaimana akibat orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan) artinya mereka binasa akibat kekafirannya itu.
t_id.iwq: Kemudian Kami utus Musa sesudah rasul-rasul itu dengan membawa ayat-ayat Kami kepada Fir'aun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya, lalu mereka mengingkari ayat-ayat itu. Maka perhatikanlah bagaimana akibat orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ثم بعثنا من بعدهم» أي الرسل المذكورين «موسى بآياتنا» التسع «إلى فرعون وملئِهِ» قومه «فظلموا» كفروا «بها فانظر كيف كان عاقبة المفسدين» بالكفر من إهلاكهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثۡنَا مِنۢ بَعۡدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِيْهِۦ فَظَلَمُواْ بِهَاۖ فَٱنظُرۡ كَيۡفَ كَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ ٱلۡمُفۡسِدِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian Kami utus sesudah rasul-rasul itu) sesudah diutusnya rasul-rasul tersebut (Musa dengan membawa ayat-ayat Kami) yang banyaknya sembilan (kepada Firaun dan pemuka-pemuka kaumnya) golongannya (lalu mereka mengingkari) mengkafiri (ayat-ayat itu. Maka perhatikanlah bagaimana akibat orang-orang yang membuat kerusakan) artinya mereka binasa akibat kekafirannya itu

ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ یَـٰفِرۡعَوۡنُ إِنِّی رَسُولࣱ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلۡعَـٰلَمِینَ
ar_simple: وقال موسى یفرعون إنی رسول من رب ٱلعلمین

t.sahih-int: And Moses said, "O Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds
t.asad: And Moses said: "O Pharaoh! Verily, I am an apostle from the Sustainer of all the worlds
t.yusuf-ali: Moses said: "O Pharaoh! I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds
t.pickthall: Moses said: O Pharaoh! Lo! I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds
t.arberry: Moses said, 'Pharaoh, I am a Messenger from the Lord of all Being
t.hilali-khan: And Musa (Moses) said: "O Fir'aun (Pharaoh)! I am a Messenger from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)
t.itani: Moses said, “O Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.”
t.mubarakpuri: And Musa said: "O Fir`awn! Verily, I am a Messenger from the Lord of all that exists."
t.qarib: Moses said, "Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds
t.wahiduddin: Moses said, "Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of the Universe
t.shakir: And Musa said: O Firon! surely I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds
t.haleem: Moses said, ‘Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of all the Worlds
t.maariful: Mūsā said, “O Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of all the worlds
t.khattab: And Moses said, “O Pharaoh! I am truly a messenger from the Lord of all worlds
t.ghali: And Musa (Moses) said, "O Firaawn! (Pharaoh) Surely I am a Messenger from The Lord of the worlds
t.irving: Moses said: "Pharaoh, I am a messenger from the Lord of the Universe
t.iwq: And Moses said, "O Pharaoh, I am a messenger from Lord of all worlds

t_id.kemenag: Dan Musa berkata: "Hai Fir'aun, sesungguhnya aku ini adalah seorang utusan dari Tuhan semesta alam,
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ berkata, "Wahai Fir'aun, sesungguhnya aku diutus oleh Allah, Tuhan semesta alam yang berkuasa mutlak atasmu, untuk menyampaikan seruan dan syariat-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Musa berkata, "Hai Firaun, sesungguhnya aku ini adalah seorang utusan Tuhan semesta alam") kepadamu, akan tetapi Firaun mendustakannya dan Musa berkata,
t_id.iwq: Dan Musa berkata: "Hai Fir'aun, sesungguhnya aku ini adalah seorang utusan dari Tuhan semesta alam,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقال موسى يا فرعون إني رسول من ربِّ العالمين» إليك فكذبه فقال أنا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَٰفِرۡعَوۡنُ إِنِّي رَسُولٞ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Musa berkata, "Hai Firaun, sesungguhnya aku ini adalah seorang utusan Tuhan semesta alam") kepadamu, akan tetapi Firaun mendustakannya dan Musa berkata

ar: حَقِیقٌ عَلَىٰۤ أَن لَّاۤ أَقُولَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلۡحَقَّۚ قَدۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِبَیِّنَةࣲ مِّن رَّبِّكُمۡ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعِیَ بَنِیۤ إِسۡرَ ٰۤءِیلَ
ar_simple: حقیق على أن لا أقول على ٱلله إلا ٱلحق قد جئتكم ببینة من ربكم فأرسل معی بنی إسر ءیل

t.sahih-int: [Who is] obligated not to say about Allah except the truth. I have come to you with clear evidence from your Lord, so send with me the Children of Israel
t.asad: so constituted that I cannot say anything about God but the truth. I have now come unto you with a clear evidence from your Sustainer: let, then, the children of Israel go with me
t.yusuf-ali: One for whom it is right to say nothing but truth about Allah. Now have I come unto you (people), from your Lord, with a clear (Sign): So let the Children of Israel depart along with me
t.pickthall: Approved upon condition that I speak concerning Allah nothing but the truth. I come unto you (lords of Egypt) with a clear proof from your Lord. So let the Children of Israel go with me
t.arberry: worthy to say nothing regarding God except the truth. I have brought a clear sign to you from your Lord; so send forth with me the Children of Israel
t.hilali-khan: Proper it is for me that I say nothing concerning Allah but the truth. Indeed I have come unto you from your Lord with a clear proof. So let the Children of Israel depart along with me
t.itani: “It is only proper that I should not say about God anything other than the truth. I have come to you with clear evidence from your Lord, so let the Children of Israel go with me.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Proper it is for me that I say nothing concerning Allah but the truth. Indeed I have come unto you from your Lord with a clear proof. So let the Children of Israel depart along with me."
t.qarib: I cannot say anything about God but the Truth. I have now come to you with clear evidence from your Lord. Send the children of Israel with me
t.wahiduddin: duty-bound to say nothing about God but the truth, and I have brought you a clear sign from your Lord. Let the Children of Israel go with me
t.shakir: (I am) worthy of not saying anything about Allah except the truth: I have come to you indeed with clear proof from your Lord, therefore send with me the children of Israel
t.haleem: duty-bound to say nothing about God but the truth, and I have brought you a clear sign from your Lord. Let the Children of Israel go with me.’
t.maariful: it befits me not to say anything about Allah except the truth. I have come to you with a clear sign from your Lord. So, let the children of Isrā’īl go with me.”
t.khattab: obliged to say nothing about Allah except the truth. Indeed, I have come to you with clear proof from your Lord, so let the children of Israel go with me.”
t.ghali: Truly (worthy) to say nothing regarding Allah except the truth. I have already come to you with a supremely evident (sign) from your Lord, so send forth with me the Seeds (Or: sons) of ."Israel
t.irving: It is (only) right for me to say nothing except the Truth about God. I have brought you an explanation from your Lord, so send the Children of Israel away with me
t.iwq: Who is obligated not to say about Allah except the truth. I have come to you with clear evidence from your Lord, so send with me the Children of Israel

t_id.kemenag: wajib atasku tidak mengatakan sesuatu terhadap Allah, kecuali yang hak. Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti yang nyata dari Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israil (pergi) bersama aku".
t_id.sabiq: Sungguh aku telah menyampaikan apa yang kudapatkan dari Allah secara benar. Untuk memperkuat kebenaran yang kubawa ini, aku datangkan kepadamu bukti-bukti yang jelas dan kuat. Maka lepaskanlah Banû Isrâ'îl pergi bersamaku. Bebaskanlah mereka dari perbudakanmu dan biarkanlah mereka keluar ke wilayah yang bukan wilayahmu, agar mereka dapat menyembah Tuhan mereka dan Tuhanmu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Aku lebih berhak) lebih pantas (untuk) agar (tidak mengatakan sesuatu terhadap Allah kecuali yang hak) menurut suatu qiraat dibaca tasydid ya-nya; haqiiqun adalah mubtada sedangkan khabarnya adalah an dan kalimat sesudahnya (Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti yang nyata dari Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah pergi bersamaku) menuju ke negeri Syam (Bani Israel.") kaum Bani Israel itu selalu ditindas oleh Firaun.
t_id.iwq: wajib atasku tidak mengatakan sesuatu terhadap Allah, kecuali yang hak. Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti yang nyata dari Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah Bani Israil (pergi) bersama aku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «حقيق» جدير «على أن» أي بأن «لا أقول على الله إلا الحق» وفي قراءة بتشديد الياء فحقيق مبتدأ خبره أن وما بعده «قد جئتكم ببينة من ربِّكم فأرسل معي» إلى الشام «بني إسرائيل» وكان استعبدهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: حَقِيقٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَن لَّآ أَقُولَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلۡحَقَّۚ قَدۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِبَيِّنَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّكُمۡ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعِيَ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ (حَقِيقٌ: جَدِيرٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Aku lebih berhak) lebih pantas (untuk) agar (tidak mengatakan sesuatu terhadap Allah kecuali yang hak) menurut suatu qiraat dibaca tasydid ya-nya; haqiiqun adalah mubtada sedangkan khabarnya adalah an dan kalimat sesudahnya (Sesungguhnya aku datang kepadamu dengan membawa bukti yang nyata dari Tuhanmu, maka lepaskanlah pergi bersamaku) menuju ke negeri Syam (Bani Israel.") kaum Bani Israel itu selalu ditindas oleh Firaun

ar: قَالَ إِن كُنتَ جِئۡتَ بِءَایَةࣲ فَأۡتِ بِهَاۤ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِینَ
ar_simple: قال إن كنت جئت بءایة فأت بها إن كنت من ٱلصدقین

t.sahih-int: [Pharaoh] said, "If you have come with a sign, then bring it forth, if you should be of the truthful
t.asad: Said [Pharaoh]: "If thou hast come with a sign, produce it-if thou art a man of truth
t.yusuf-ali: (Pharaoh) said: "If indeed thou hast come with a Sign, show it forth,- if thou tellest the truth
t.pickthall: (Pharaoh) said: If thou comest with a token, then produce it, if thou art of those who speak the truth
t.arberry: Said he, 'If thou hast brought a sign, produce it, if thou speakest truly
t.hilali-khan: Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said: "If you have come with a sign, show it forth, - if you are one of those who tell the truth
t.itani: He said, “If you brought a miracle, then present it, if you are truthful.”
t.mubarakpuri: [Fir`awn] said: "If you have come with a sign, show it forth, if you are one of those who tell the truth."
t.qarib: [Pharaoh] said, "If you have come with a sign, produce it, if you are a man of truth
t.wahiduddin: [Pharaoh] said, "If you come with a sign, then produce it, if you are telling the truth
t.shakir: He said: If you have come with a sign, then bring it, if you are of the truthful ones
t.haleem: He said, ‘Produce this sign you have brought, if you are telling the truth.’
t.maariful: He said, “If you have come with a sign, then bring it forth, if you are really truthful.”
t.khattab: Pharaoh said, “If you have come with a sign, then bring it if what you say is true.”
t.ghali: Said he, "In case you have come with a sign, so come up with it, in case you are of the sincere
t.irving: He said: ´If you have brought some sign, well produce it then if you are so truthful
t.iwq: Pharaoh said, "If you have come with a sign, then bring it forth, if you should be of the truthful

t_id.kemenag: Fir'aun menjawab: "Jika benar kamu membawa sesuatu bukti, maka datangkanlah bukti itu jika (betul) kamu termasuk orang-orang yang benar".
t_id.sabiq: Fir'aun menjawab, "Kalau kamu memang datang dengan membawa bukti pendukung dari Pengutusmu, maka perlihatkanlah itu kepadaku, jika kamu memang termasuk orang yang dapat dipercaya karena selalu mengatakan kebenaran."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Firaun kepadanya, ("Jika kamu benar membawa sesuatu ayat) bukti yang memperkuat pengakuanmu (maka datangkanlah bukti itu jika betul kamu termasuk orang-orang yang benar.") membawa bukti itu.
t_id.iwq: Fir'aun menjawab: "Jika benar kamu membawa sesuatu bukti, maka datangkanlah bukti itu jika (betul) kamu termasuk orang-orang yang benar".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» فرعون له «إن كنت جئت بآية» على دعواك «فأت بها إن كنت من الصادقين» فيها
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ إِن كُنتَ جِئۡتَ بِـَٔايَةٖ فَأۡتِ بِهَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah) Firaun kepadanya, ("Jika kamu benar membawa sesuatu ayat) bukti yang memperkuat pengakuanmu (maka datangkanlah bukti itu jika betul kamu termasuk orang-orang yang benar.") membawa bukti itu

ar: فَأَلۡقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِیَ ثُعۡبَانࣱ مُّبِینࣱ
ar_simple: فألقى عصاه فإذا هی ثعبان مبین

t.sahih-int: So Moses threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent, manifest
t.asad: Thereupon [Moses] threw down his staff, and lo! it was a serpent, plainly visible
t.yusuf-ali: Then (Moses) threw his rod, and behold! it was a serpent, plain (for all to see)
t.pickthall: Then he flung down his staff and lo! it was a serpent manifest
t.arberry: So he cast his staff; and behold, it was a serpent manifest
t.hilali-khan: Then [Musa (Moses)] threw his stick and behold! it was a serpent, manifest
t.itani: So he threw his staff, and it was an apparent serpent.
t.mubarakpuri: Then [Musa] threw his staff and behold! it was a [Thu`ban] serpent, manifest!
t.qarib: [Moses] threw down his staff, and it was clearly a snake
t.wahiduddin: Then Moses threw down his staff and suddenly, unmistakably, it appeared as a serpent
t.shakir: So he threw his rod, then lo! it was a clear serpent
t.haleem: So Moses threw his staff and- lo and behold!- it was a snake, clear to all
t.maariful: So he threw down his staff, and in no time it was a vivid serpent
t.khattab: So Moses threw down his staff and—behold!—it became a real snake
t.ghali: So he cast his staff, and only then was it an evident serpent
t.irving: He tossed his staff down and imagine, it obviously became a snake
t.iwq: Thus Moses threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent, manifest

t_id.kemenag: Maka Musa menjatuhkan tongkat-nya, lalu seketika itu juga tongkat itu menjadi ular yang sebenarnya.
t_id.sabiq: Maka Mûsâ pun segera melemparkan tongkat yang ada di tangan kanannya ke hadapan Fir'aun dan kaumnya. Tiba-tiba tongkat itu menjadi ular yang merayap dengan cepat ke sana ke mari, yang menunjukkan bahwa ular itu memang benar-benar hidup.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Musa menjatuhkan tongkatnya, lalu seketika itu juga tongkat itu menjadi ular yang sebenarnya) yakni ular yang sangat besar bentuknya.
t_id.iwq: Maka Musa menjatuhkan tongkat-nya, lalu seketika itu juga tongkat itu menjadi ular yang sebenarnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فألقى عصاه فإذا هي ثعبان مبين» حية عظيمة
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَلۡقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ ثُعۡبَانٞ مُّبِينٞ (ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ: حَيَّةٌ عَظِيمَةٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Musa menjatuhkan tongkatnya, lalu seketika itu juga tongkat itu menjadi ular yang sebenarnya) yakni ular yang sangat besar bentuknya

ar: وَنَزَعَ یَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِیَ بَیۡضَاۤءُ لِلنَّـٰظِرِینَ
ar_simple: ونزع یده فإذا هی بیضاء للنظرین

t.sahih-int: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white [with radiance] for the observers
t.asad: and he drew forth his hand, and lo! it appeared [shining] white to the beholders
t.yusuf-ali: And he drew out his hand, and behold! it was white to all beholders
t.pickthall: And he drew forth his hand (from his bosom), and lo! it was white for the beholders
t.arberry: And he drew forth his hand, and lo, it was white to the beholders
t.hilali-khan: And he drew out his hand, and behold! it was white (with radiance) for the beholders
t.itani: And He pulled out his hand, and it was white to the onlookers.
t.mubarakpuri: And he drew out his hand, and behold! it was white (with radiance) for the beholders.
t.qarib: He pulled out his hand and it was white for all to see
t.wahiduddin: and he drew forth his hand, and it appeared [shining] white to the beholders
t.shakir: And he drew forth his hand, and lo! it was white to the beholders
t.haleem: and then he pulled out his hand and- lo and behold!- it was white for all to see
t.maariful: and he drew out his hand, and it was luminous for the onlookers
t.khattab: Then he drew his hand ˹out of his collar˺ and it was ˹shining˺ white for all to see
t.ghali: And he drew out his hand, and only then was it white to the on-lookers
t.irving: He pulled out his hand (from his shirtfront), and imagine, it was white to the onlookers
t.iwq: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white with radiance for the observers

t_id.kemenag: Dan ia mengeluarkan tangannya, maka ketika itu juga tangan itu menjadi putih bercahaya (kelihatan) oleh orang-orang yang melihatnya.
t_id.sabiq: Dan ia pun mengeluarkan tangan dari kantongnya. Saat itu tangannya menjadi putih gemerlapan, membuat silau orang-orang yang melihatnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ia mengeluarkan tangannya) mengeluarkannya dari dalam sakunya (maka ketika itu juga tangan itu menjadi putih bercahaya menyilaukan (bagi orang-orang yang melihatnya) berbeda warnanya dengan keadaan kulit tangan yang sebenarnya.
t_id.iwq: Dan ia mengeluarkan tangannya, maka ketika itu juga tangan itu menjadi putih bercahaya (kelihatan) oleh orang-orang yang melihatnya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونزع يده» أخرجها من جيبه «فإذا هي بيضاء» ذات شعاع «للناظرين» خلاف ما كانت عليه من الأدمة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِيَ بَيۡضَآءُ لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ (وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُ: نَزَعَهَا مِنْ جَيْبِهِ، أَوْ جَنَاحِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ia mengeluarkan tangannya) mengeluarkannya dari dalam sakunya (maka ketika itu juga tangan itu menjadi putih bercahaya menyilaukan (bagi orang-orang yang melihatnya) berbeda warnanya dengan keadaan kulit tangan yang sebenarnya

ar: قَالَ ٱلۡمَلَأُ مِن قَوۡمِ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌ عَلِیمࣱ
ar_simple: قال ٱلملأ من قوم فرعون إن هذا لسحر علیم

t.sahih-int: Said the eminent among the people of Pharaoh, "Indeed, this is a learned magician
t.asad: The great ones among Pharaoh's people said: "Verily, this is indeed a sorcerer of great knowledge
t.yusuf-ali: Said the Chiefs of the people of Pharaoh: "This is indeed a sorcerer well-versed
t.pickthall: The chiefs of Pharaoh's people said: Lo! this is some knowing wizard
t.arberry: Said the Council of the people of Pharaoh, 'Surely this man is a cunning sorcerer
t.hilali-khan: The chiefs of the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "This is indeed a well-versed sorcerer
t.itani: The notables among Pharaoh’s people said, “This is really a skilled magician.”
t.mubarakpuri: The chiefs of the people of Fir`awn said: "This is indeed a well-versed sorcerer."
t.qarib: The notables among Pharaoh's people said, "This is definitely a sorcerer with great knowledge
t.wahiduddin: The chiefs of Pharaoh's people said, "This is most surely a skillful magician
t.shakir: The chiefs of Firon's people said: most surely this is an enchanter possessed of knowledge
t.haleem: The leaders among Pharaoh’s people said, ‘This man is a learned sorcerer
t.maariful: The chiefs of the people of Pharaoh said, “This man is certainly a sorcerer of great knowledge
t.khattab: The chiefs of Pharaoh’s people said, “He is indeed a skilled magician
t.ghali: The chiefs of Firaawn's (pharaoh's) people said, "Surely this is indeed a knowledgeable sorcerer
t.irving: The notables among Pharaoh´s people said: ´This is some clever magician
t.iwq: Said the eminent among the people of Pharaoh, "Indeed, this is a learned magician

t_id.kemenag: Pemuka-pemuka kaum Fir'aun berkata: "Sesungguhnya Musa ini adalah ahli sihir yang pandai,
t_id.sabiq: Maka ketika Mûsâ telah memperlihatkan bukti-bukti dari Allah itu, tercenganglah para kroni dan pembesar Fir'aun. Mereka pun, dengan menjilat dan bersikap munafik, berkata kepada Fir'aun, "Ini sebenarnya hanya kemahiran sihir saja, bukan bukti dari Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Pemuka-pemuka kaum Firaun berkata, "Sesungguhnya Musa ini adalah ahli sihir yang pandai) yang ulung di dalam ilmu sihir, dan di dalam surah Asy-Syu`ara disebutkan bahwa perkataan ini adalah perkataan Firaun sendiri. Seolah-olah para pemuka kaum Firaun itu mengatakan perkataan tersebut bersama Firaun sendiri, setelah mereka dan dia bermusyawarah tentang hal itu.
t_id.iwq: Pemuka-pemuka kaum Fir'aun berkata: "Sesungguhnya Musa ini adalah ahli sihir yang pandai,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال الملأ من قوم فرعون إنَّ هذا لساحر عليم» فائق في علم السحر. وفي الشعراء أنه من قول فرعون نفسه فكأنهم قالوه معه على سبيل التشاور
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ ٱلۡمَلَأُ مِن قَوۡمِ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pemuka-pemuka kaum Firaun berkata, "Sesungguhnya Musa ini adalah ahli sihir yang pandai) yang ulung di dalam ilmu sihir, dan di dalam surah Asy-Syu`ara disebutkan bahwa perkataan ini adalah perkataan Firaun sendiri. Seolah-olah para pemuka kaum Firaun itu mengatakan perkataan tersebut bersama Firaun sendiri, setelah mereka dan dia bermusyawarah tentang hal itu

ar: یُرِیدُ أَن یُخۡرِجَكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُمۡۖ فَمَاذَا تَأۡمُرُونَ
ar_simple: یرید أن یخرجكم من أرضكم فماذا تأمرون

t.sahih-int: Who wants to expel you from your land [through magic], so what do you instruct
t.asad: who wants to drive you out of your land! " [Said Pharaoh:] "What, then, do you advise
t.yusuf-ali: His plan is to get you out of your land: then what is it ye counsel
t.pickthall: Who would expel you from your land. Now what do ye advise
t.arberry: who desires to expel you from your land; what do you command
t.hilali-khan: He wants to get you out of your land, so what do you advise
t.itani: “He wants to evict you from your land, so what do you recommend?”
t.mubarakpuri: "He wants to get you out of your land, so what do you advise"
t.qarib: who wants to drive you out of your land. [Pharaoh said], "What do you advise
t.wahiduddin: who seeks to drive you from your land!" Pharaoh said, "What then do you advise
t.shakir: He intends to turn you out of your land. What counsel do you then give
t.haleem: He means to drive you out of your land!’ Pharaoh said, ‘What do you suggest?’
t.maariful: He wants to expel you from your land. So, what do you suggest?”
t.khattab: who seeks to drive you from your land.” ˹So Pharaoh asked,˺ “What do you propose?”
t.ghali: Who would (like to) drive you out of your land; so, what (is it that) you command
t.irving: He wants to drive you out of your land; so what do you command
t.iwq: Who wants to expel you from your land through magic, so what do you instruct

t_id.kemenag: yang bermaksud hendak mengeluarkan kamu dari negerimu". (Fir'aun berkata): "Maka apakah yang kamu anjurkan?"
t_id.sabiq: Dengan sihirnya itu, ia sebenarnya ingin merampas kekuasaanmu dan mengusirmu dari negeri ini. Dengan sihir itu pula ia dapat meluluhkan hati warga negeri ini agar mengikutinya. Fir'aun berkata, "Cobalah pikirkan apa saran kalian untuk keluar dari kesulitan yang dilakukannya ini!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yang bermaksud hendak mengeluarkan kamu dari negerimu." Firaun berkata, "Maka apakah yang kamu anjurkan?")
t_id.iwq: yang bermaksud hendak mengeluarkan kamu dari negerimu". (Fir'aun berkata): "Maka apakah yang kamu anjurkan?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يريد أن يخرجكم من أرضكم فماذا تأمرون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: يُرِيدُ أَن يُخۡرِجَكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُمۡۖ فَمَاذَا تَأۡمُرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Yang bermaksud hendak mengeluarkan kamu dari negerimu." Firaun berkata, "Maka apakah yang kamu anjurkan

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ أَرۡجِهۡ وَأَخَاهُ وَأَرۡسِلۡ فِی ٱلۡمَدَاۤئِنِ حَـٰشِرِینَ
ar_simple: قالوا أرجه وأخاه وأرسل فی ٱلمدائن حشرین

t.sahih-int: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers

t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Keep him and his brother in suspense (for a while); and send to the cities men to collect

t.arberry: They said, 'Put him and his brother off a while, and send among the cities musterers
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Put him and his brother off (for a time), and send callers (men) to the cities to collect (and)

t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Put him and his brother off (for a time), and send callers to the cities to collect"
t.qarib: They answered, "Let him and his brother wait a while, and send messengers to all cities
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Let him and his brother wait awhile, and send into the cities summoners

t.haleem: They said, ‘Delay him and his brother for a while, and send messengers to all the cities
t.maariful: They said, “leave him and his brother alone for a while, and send (your) men to the cities to collect
t.khattab: They replied, “Let him and his brother wait and send mobilizers to all cities
t.ghali: They said, "Put him off, and his brother, (a while) and send among the cities mutterers

ar: وَجَاۤءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ فِرۡعَوۡنَ قَالُوۤا۟ إِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجۡرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحۡنُ ٱلۡغَـٰلِبِینَ

t_id.kemenag: Dan beberapa ahli sihir itu datang kepada Fir'aun mengatakan: "(Apakah) sesungguhnya kami akan mendapat upah, jika kamilah yang menang?"
t_id.sabiq: Ahli-ahli sihir yang dikumpulkan oleh bala tentara itu pun datang ke hadapan Fir'aun. Kemudian mereka berkata kepada Fir'aun, "Tentulah kami akan mendapatkan upah yang besar--sesuai dengan tugas yang kami emban--jika kami dapat mengalahkan Mûsâ."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan beberapa ahli sihir itu datang kepada Firaun mengatakan, "Apakah sesungguhnya) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal inna dibaca ainna (kami akan mendapat upah jika kamilah yang menang?").
t_id.iwq: Dan beberapa ahli sihir itu datang kepada Fir'aun mengatakan: "(Apakah) sesungguhnya kami akan mendapat upah, jika kamilah yang menang?"

tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan beberapa ahli sihir itu datang kepada Firaun mengatakan, "Apakah sesungguhnya) menurut qiraat yang lain lafal inna dibaca ainna (kami akan mendapat upah jika kamilah yang menang)

ar: قَالَ نَعَمۡ وَإِنَّكُمۡ لَمِنَ ٱلۡمُقَرَّبِینَ
ar_simple: قال نعم وإنكم لمن ٱلمقربین

ar: قَالُوا۟ یَـٰمُوسَىٰۤ إِمَّاۤ أَن تُلۡقِیَ وَإِمَّاۤ أَن نَّكُونَ نَحۡنُ ٱلۡمُلۡقِینَ

t_id.kemenag: Ahli-ahli sihir berkata: "Hai Musa, kamukah yang akan melemparkan lebih dahulu, ataukah kami yang akan melemparkan?"
t_id.sabiq: Para ahli sihir itu pun--setelah mendapatkan janji Fir'aun--dengan penuh percaya diri mendatangi Mûsâ dan berkata, "Silakan lempar lebih dulu apa yang kamu miliki, atau biar kami yang lebih dulu melempar."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ahli-ahli sihir berkata, "Hai Musa! Kamukah yang akan melemparkan lebih dahulu) tongkatmu (ataukah kami yang akan melemparkan?") apa-apa yang ada pada kami.
t_id.iwq: Ahli-ahli sihir berkata: "Hai Musa, kamukah yang akan melemparkan lebih dahulu, ataukah kami yang akan melemparkan?"

tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ahli-ahli sihir berkata, "Hai Musa! Kamukah yang akan melemparkan lebih dahulu) tongkatmu (ataukah kami yang akan melemparkan?") apa-apa yang ada pada kami

ar: قَالَ أَلۡقُوا۟ۖ فَلَمَّاۤ أَلۡقَوۡا۟ سَحَرُوۤا۟ أَعۡیُنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱسۡتَرۡهَبُوهُمۡ وَجَاۤءُو بِسِحۡرٍ عَظِیمࣲ
ar_simple: قال ألقوا فلما ألقوا سحروا أعین ٱلناس وٱسترهبوهم وجاءو بسحر عظیم

t.sahih-int: He said, "Throw," and when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people and struck terror into them, and they presented a great [feat of] magic
t.asad: He answered: "You throw [first]." And when they threw down [their staffs], they cast a spell upon the people's eyes, and struck them with awe, and produced mighty sorcery
t.yusuf-ali: Said Moses: "Throw ye (first)." So when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, and struck terror into them: for they showed a great (feat of) magic
t.pickthall: He said: Throw! And when they threw they cast a spell upon the people's eyes, and overawed them, and produced a mighty spell
t.arberry: He said, 'You cast.' And when they cast they put a Spell upon the people's eyes, and called forth fear of them, and produced a mighty sorcery
t.hilali-khan: He [Musa (Moses)] said: "Throw you (first)." So when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, and struck terror into them, and they displayed a great magic
t.itani: He said, “You throw!” And when they threw, they beguiled the eyes of the people, and intimidated them, and produced a mighty magic.
t.mubarakpuri: He [Musa] said: "Throw you (first)." So when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, and struck terror into them, and they displayed a great trick.
t.qarib: He answered, "You throw [first]."When they threw down [their staffs], they cast a spell upon the people's eyes, and struck them with fear, producing mighty sorcery
t.wahiduddin: He said, "You throw [first]!" When they made their cast, they bewitched the eyes of the people and struck them with awe, for they showed a great [feat of] magic
t.shakir: He said: Cast. So when they cast, they deceived the people's eyes and frightened them, and they produced a mighty enchantment
t.haleem: He said, ‘You throw,’ and they did, casting a spell on people’s eyes, striking fear into them, and bringing about great sorcery
t.maariful: He said, “You throw.” So when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people, and made them frightened, and produced great sorcery
t.khattab: Moses said, “You first.” So when they did, they deceived the eyes of the people, stunned them, and made a great display of magic
t.ghali: Said he, "You cast." So as soon as they cast, they bewitched (their) fellowmen?s eyes, and overawed them, and came with a tremendous sorcery
t.irving: He said: "You throw [first]!" So when they threw, they charmed the people´s eyes and overawed them. They produced a splendid trick
t.iwq: He said, "Throw," and when they threw, they bewitched the eyes of the people and struck terror into them, and they presented a great feat of magic

t_id.kemenag: Musa menjawab: "Lemparkanlah (lebih dahulu)!" Maka tatkala mereka melemparkan, mereka menyulap mata orang dan menjadikan orang banyak itu takut, serta mereka mendatangkan sihir yang besar (mena'jubkan).
t_id.sabiq: Dengan penuh keyakinan dan tanpa rasa takut sedikit pun, Mûsâ menjawab, "Lemparlah lebih dulu apa yang ada pada kalian." Ketika mereka semua telah melempar apa yang dibawa berupa tali dan tongkat, mata orang-orang pun terkelabui, seolah-olah apa yang mereka lakukan itu benar-benar terjadi. Padahal sebenarnya hal itu merupakan kamuflase belaka. Orang-orang pun tercengang dan ketakutan. Para ahli sihir itu memang telah melakukan suatu bentuk sihir yang hebat dan mengagumkan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Musa menjawab, "Lemparkanlah olehmu lebih dahulu) ini adalah suatu perintah yang mempersilakan mereka untuk melemparkan apa yang ada pada mereka, sebagai suatu taktik dari Musa untuk menampakkan yang hak (Maka tatkala mereka melemparkan) tambang-tambang mereka dan tongkat-tongkat mereka (mereka menyulap mata orang) mereka membalik mata para hadirin supaya tidak bisa melihat hal yang sebenarnya (dan menjadikan orang banyak itu takut) artinya mereka membuatnya takut karena mereka menjadikan seolah-olah hal itu adalah ular-ular yang menjalar (serta mereka mendatangkan sihir yang besar, menakjubkan").
t_id.iwq: Musa menjawab: "Lemparkanlah (lebih dahulu)!" Maka tatkala mereka melemparkan, mereka menyulap mata orang dan menjadikan orang banyak itu takut, serta mereka mendatangkan sihir yang besar (mena'jubkan).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال ألقوا» أمر للإذن بتقديم إلقائهم توصلا به إلى إظهار الحق «فلما ألقوا» حبالهم وعصيهم «سحروا أعين الناس» صرفوها عن حقيقة إدراكها «واسترهبوهم» خوفوهم حيث خيلوها حيات تسعى «وجاءوا بسحر عظيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ أَلۡقُواْۖ فَلَمَّآ أَلۡقَوۡاْ سَحَرُوٓاْ أَعۡيُنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱسۡتَرۡهَبُوهُمۡ وَجَآءُو بِسِحۡرٍ عَظِيمٖ (وَاسْتَرْهَبُوهُمْ: خَوَّفُوهُمْ، وَأَرْهَبُوهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Musa menjawab, "Lemparkanlah olehmu lebih dahulu) ini adalah suatu perintah yang mempersilakan mereka untuk melemparkan apa yang ada pada mereka, sebagai suatu taktik dari Musa untuk menampakkan yang hak (Maka tatkala mereka melemparkan) tambang-tambang mereka dan tongkat-tongkat mereka (mereka menyulap mata orang) mereka membalik mata para hadirin supaya tidak bisa melihat hal yang sebenarnya (dan menjadikan orang banyak itu takut) artinya mereka membuatnya takut karena mereka menjadikan seolah-olah hal itu adalah ular-ular yang menjalar (serta mereka mendatangkan sihir yang besar, menakjubkan)

ar: ۞ وَأَوۡحَیۡنَاۤ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰۤ أَنۡ أَلۡقِ عَصَاكَۖ فَإِذَا هِیَ تَلۡقَفُ مَا یَأۡفِكُونَ
ar_simple: وأوحینا إلى موسى أن ألق عصاك فإذا هی تلقف ما یأفكون

t.sahih-int: And We inspired to Moses, "Throw your staff," and at once it devoured what they were falsifying
t.asad: And [then] We inspired Moses, "Throw down thy staff!" -and lo! it swallowed up all their deceptions
t.yusuf-ali: We put it into Moses's mind by inspiration: "Throw (now) thy rod": and behold! it swallows up straight away all the falsehoods which they fake
t.pickthall: And We inspired Moses (saying): Throw thy staff! And lo! it swallowed up their lying show
t.arberry: And We revealed to Moses: 'Cast thy staff!' And lo, it forthwith swallowed up their lying invention
t.hilali-khan: And We inspired Musa (Moses) (saying): "Throw your stick," and behold! It swallowed up straight away all the falsehoods which they showed
t.itani: And We inspired Moses: “Throw your staff.” And at once, it swallowed what they were faking.
t.mubarakpuri: And We revealed to Musa (saying): "Throw your stick," and behold! It swallowed up straight away all the falsehood which they showed.
t.qarib: [Then] We inspired Moses, "Throw down your staff," and it swallowed up all their deceptions
t.wahiduddin: Then We inspired Moses, saying, "Throw down your staff." And it immediately swallowed up their false devices
t.shakir: And We revealed to Musa, saying: Cast your rod; then lo! it devoured the lies they told
t.haleem: Then We inspired Moses, ‘Throw your staff,’ and- lo and behold!––it devoured their fakery
t.maariful: We revealed to Mūsā, “Throw your staff.” Then of a sudden, it began to swallow all that they had concocted
t.khattab: Then We inspired Moses, “Throw down your staff,” and—behold!—it devoured the objects of their illusion
t.ghali: And We revealed to Musa (Moses) (saying), "Cast your staff." So, only then did it gulp whatever they falsified
t.irving: We inspired Moses with: ´Throw your staff," and imagine, it swallowed up whatever they had trumped up
t.iwq: And We inspired to Moses, "Throw your staff," and at once it devoured what they were falsifying

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami wahyukan kepada Musa: "Lemparkanlah tongkatmu!". Maka sekonyong-konyong tongkat itu menelan apa yang mereka sulapkan.
t_id.sabiq: Allah pun memerintahkan agar Mûsâ melempar tongkatnya. Tibalah saat yang ditunggu, dan Mûsâ pun segera melemparkan tongkatnya. Seketika itu juga tongkat itu menelan dengan cepat pengelabuan dan kebohongan sihir mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami wahyukan kepada Musa, "Lemparkanlah tongkatmu!" Maka sekonyong-konyong tongkat itu menelan) dengan membuang salah satu di antara kedua ta yang asal, yakni: tongkat itu mencaplok (apa yang mereka sulapkan) apa yang mereka balikkan pada pandangan mata orang dengan tipu sulap mereka.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami wahyukan kepada Musa: "Lemparkanlah tongkatmu!". Maka sekonyong-konyong tongkat itu menelan apa yang mereka sulapkan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأوحينا إلى موسى أن ألق عصاك فإذا هي تلقف» بحذف إحدى التاءين في الأصل تبتلع «ما يأفكون» يقلبون بتمويههم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞وَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنۡ أَلۡقِ عَصَاكَۖ فَإِذَا هِيَ تَلۡقَفُ مَا يَأۡفِكُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami wahyukan kepada Musa, "Lemparkanlah tongkatmu!" Maka sekonyong-konyong tongkat itu menelan) dengan membuang salah satu di antara kedua ta yang asal, yakni: tongkat itu mencaplok (apa yang mereka sulapkan) apa yang mereka balikkan pada pandangan mata orang dengan tipu sulap mereka

ar: فَوَقَعَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَبَطَلَ مَا كَانُوا۟ یَعۡمَلُونَ
ar_simple: فوقع ٱلحق وبطل ما كانوا یعملون

t.sahih-int: So the truth was established, and abolished was what they were doing
t.asad: whereupon the truth was established, and vain was proved all that they had been doing
t.yusuf-ali: Thus truth was confirmed, and all that they did was made of no effect
t.pickthall: Thus was the Truth vindicated and that which they were doing was made vain
t.arberry: So the truth came to pass, and false was proved what they were doing
t.hilali-khan: Thus truth was confirmed, and all that they did was made of no effect
t.itani: So the truth came to pass, and what they were producing came to nothing.
t.mubarakpuri: Thus truth was confirmed, and all that they did was made of no effect.
t.qarib: The Truth was confirmed and what they had produced came to nothing
t.wahiduddin: Thus the truth prevailed and what they had produced came to nothing
t.shakir: So the truth was established, and what they did became null
t.haleem: The truth was confirmed and what they had produced came to nothing
t.maariful: Thus the truth prevailed, and what they were doing became a nullity
t.khattab: So the truth prevailed and their illusions failed
t.ghali: So the Truth came to pass, (Literally: fell "on them") and void (Literally: became untrue) was whatever they were doing
t.irving: Truth prevailed and what they had been doing collapsed
t.iwq: Thus the truth was established, and abolished was what they were doing

t_id.kemenag: Karena itu nyatalah yang benar dan batallah yang selalu mereka kerjakan.
t_id.sabiq: Maka nyatalah kebenaran yang berada di pihak Mûsâ dan batillah pengelabuan yang dilakukan para ahli sihir itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Karena itu nyatalah yang benar) yakni telah tetap dan menang yang benar itu (dan batallah yang selalu mereka kerjakan) yaitu perbuatan-perbuatan sihir mereka.
t_id.iwq: Karena itu nyatalah yang benar dan batallah yang selalu mereka kerjakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فوقع الحق» ثبت وظهر «وبطل ما كانوا يعملون» من السحر
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَوَقَعَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَبَطَلَ مَا كَانُواْ يَعۡمَلُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Karena itu nyatalah yang benar) yakni telah tetap dan menang yang benar itu (dan batallah yang selalu mereka kerjakan) yaitu perbuatan-perbuatan sihir mereka

ar: فَغُلِبُوا۟ هُنَالِكَ وَٱنقَلَبُوا۟ صَـٰغِرِینَ
ar_simple: فغلبوا هنالك وٱنقلبوا صغرین

t.sahih-int: And Pharaoh and his people were overcome right there and became debased
t.asad: And thus were they vanquished there and then, and became utterly humiliated
t.yusuf-ali: So the (great ones) were vanquished there and then, and were made to look small
t.pickthall: Thus were they there defeated and brought low
t.arberry: So they were vanquished there, and they turned about, humbled
t.hilali-khan: So they were defeated there and then, and were returned disgraced
t.itani: There they were defeated, and utterly reduced.
t.mubarakpuri: So they were defeated there and returned disgraced.
t.qarib: They were vanquished there and then, and they became utterly humiliated
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh and his men were defeated and utterly humiliated
t.shakir: Thus they were vanquished there, and they went back abased
t.haleem: they were defeated there and utterly humiliated
t.maariful: So, they were overcome then and there and turned humiliated
t.khattab: So Pharaoh and his people were defeated right there and put to shame
t.ghali: So they were overcome over there, and they turned over belittled
t.irving: those men were defeated and were turned back belittled
t.iwq: And Pharaoh and his people were overcome right there and became debased

t_id.kemenag: Maka mereka kalah di tempat itu dan jadilah mereka orang-orang yang hina.
t_id.sabiq: Dalam pertemuan itu, Fir'aun beserta para pembantunya berada di pihak yang kalah. Mereka merasa sangat terhina dengan kekalahan ini.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka kalah) yakni Firaun dan kaumnya (di tempat itu dan jadilah mereka orang-orang yang hina) artinya kini mereka menjadi orang-orang yang kecil lagi hina.
t_id.iwq: Maka mereka kalah di tempat itu dan jadilah mereka orang-orang yang hina.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فغُلبوا» أي فرعون وقومه «هنالك وانقلبوا صاغرين» صاروا ذليلين
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَغُلِبُواْ هُنَالِكَ وَٱنقَلَبُواْ صَٰغِرِينَ (وَانقَلَبُوا: انْصَرَفُوا, صَاغِرِينَ: أَذِلَّاءَ، مَقْهُورِينَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka kalah) yakni Firaun dan kaumnya (di tempat itu dan jadilah mereka orang-orang yang hina) artinya kini mereka menjadi orang-orang yang kecil lagi hina

ar: وَأُلۡقِیَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سَـٰجِدِینَ
ar_simple: وألقی ٱلسحرة سجدین

t.sahih-int: And the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allah]
t.asad: And down fell the sorcerers, prostrating themselves
t.yusuf-ali: But the sorcerers fell down prostrate in adoration
t.pickthall: And the wizards fell down prostrate
t.arberry: And the sorcerers were cast down, bowing themselves
t.hilali-khan: And the sorcerers fell down prostrate
t.itani: And the magicians fell to their knees.
t.mubarakpuri: And the sorcerers fell down prostrate.
t.qarib: The sorcerers fell down bowing themselves
t.wahiduddin: And the sorcerers prostrated themselves
t.shakir: And the enchanters were thrown down, prostrating (themselves)
t.haleem: The sorcerers fell to their knees
t.maariful: The sorcerers could not but fall in prostration
t.khattab: And the magicians fell down, prostrating
t.ghali: And the sorcerers were cast down prostrating
t.irving: The magicians dropped down on their knees
t.iwq: And the magicians fell down in prostration to Allah

t_id.kemenag: Dan ahli-ahli sihir itu serta merta meniarapkan diri dengan bersujud.
t_id.sabiq: Demikianlah halnya Fir'aun dan pembesar-pembesarnya. Adapun para ahli sihir itu, mereka tercengang kagum mendapati kebenaran yang terjadi itu, sehingga terdorong untuk sujud kepada Allah dan tunduk kepada kebenaran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ahli-ahli sihir itu dengan serta-merta meniarapkan diri dengan bersujud).
t_id.iwq: Dan ahli-ahli sihir itu serta merta meniarapkan diri dengan bersujud.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأُلقي السحرة ساجدين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأُلۡقِيَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سَٰجِدِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Dan ahli-ahli sihir itu dengan serta-merta meniarapkan diri dengan bersujud

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلۡعَـٰلَمِینَ
ar_simple: قالوا ءامنا برب ٱلعلمین

t.sahih-int: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds
t.asad: [and] exclaiming: "We have come to believe in the Sustainer of all the worlds
t.yusuf-ali: Saying: "We believe in the Lord of the Worlds
t.pickthall: Crying: We believe in the Lord of the Worlds
t.arberry: They said, 'We believe in the Lord of all Being
t.hilali-khan: They said: "We believe in the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)
t.itani: They said, “We have believed in the Lord of the Worlds.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "We believe in the Lord of all that exists.
t.qarib: [and] said, "We have come to believe in the Lord of the worlds
t.wahiduddin: and said, "We believe in the Lord of the Universe
t.shakir: They said: We believe in the Lord of the worlds
t.haleem: and said, ‘We believe in the Lord of the Worlds
t.maariful: They said, “We believe in the Lord of the worlds
t.khattab: They declared, “We ˹now˺ believe in the Lord of all worlds—
t.ghali: They said, "We believe in The Lord of the worlds
t.irving: they said: "We believe in the Lord of the Universe
t.iwq: And they said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Kami beriman kepada Tuhan semesta alam,
t_id.sabiq: Mereka berkata, "Kami beriman kepada Pencipta alam semesta yang menguasai serta mengaturnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Kami beriman kepada Tuhan semesta alam).
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Kami beriman kepada Tuhan semesta alam,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا آمنا برب العالمين»

ar: رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ
ar_simple: رب موسى وهرون

t.sahih-int: The Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.asad: the Sustainer of Moses and Aaron
t.yusuf-ali: The Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.pickthall: The Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.arberry: the Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.hilali-khan: The Lord of Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron)
t.itani: “The Lord of Moses and Aaron.”
t.mubarakpuri: "The Lord of Musa and Harun."
t.qarib: the Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.wahiduddin: the Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.shakir: The Lord of Musa and Haroun
t.haleem: the Lord of Moses and Aaron!’
t.maariful: the Lord of Mūsā and Hārūn.”
t.khattab: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.”
t.ghali: The Lord of Musa (Moses) and Harun." (Aaron)
t.irving: the Lord of Moses and Aaron
t.iwq: The Lord of Moses and Aaron

t_id.kemenag: "(yaitu) Tuhan Musa dan Harun".
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Dia adalah Tuhan yang diyakini dan diimani oleh Mûsâ dan Hârûn."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu Tuhan Musa dan Harun") berkat pengetahuan mereka yang menyimpulkan bahwa apa yang telah mereka saksikan itu, yaitu tentang tongkat Musa semata-mata bukanlah perbuatan sihir.
t_id.iwq: "(yaitu) Tuhan Musa dan Harun".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «رب موسى وهارون» لعلمهم بأن ما شهدوه من العصا لا يتأتى بالسحر

ar: قَالَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَمَكۡرࣱ مَّكَرۡتُمُوهُ فِی ٱلۡمَدِینَةِ لِتُخۡرِجُوا۟ مِنۡهَاۤ أَهۡلَهَاۖ فَسَوۡفَ تَعۡلَمُونَ
ar_simple: قال فرعون ءامنتم به قبل أن ءاذن لكم إن هذا لمكر مكرتموه فی ٱلمدینة لتخرجوا منها أهلها فسوف تعلمون

t.sahih-int: Said Pharaoh, "You believed in him before I gave you permission. Indeed, this is a conspiracy which you conspired in the city to expel therefrom its people. But you are going to know
t.asad: Said Pharaoh: "Have you come to believe in him ere I have given you permission? Behold, this is indeed a plot which you have cunningly devised in this [my] city in order to drive out its people hence! But in time you shall come to know, [my revenge]
t.yusuf-ali: Said Pharaoh: "Believe ye in Him before I give you permission? Surely this is a trick which ye have planned in the city to drive out its people: but soon shall ye know (the consequences)
t.pickthall: Pharaoh said: Ye believe in Him before I give you leave! Lo! this is the plot that ye have plotted in the city that ye may drive its people hence. But ye shall come to know
t.arberry: Said Pharaoh, 'You have believed in Him before I gave you leave. Surely this is a device you have devised in the city that you may expel its people from it. Now you shall know
t.hilali-khan: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "You have believed in him [Musa (Moses)] before I give you permission. Surely, this is a plot which you have plotted in the city to drive out its people, but you shall come to know
t.itani: Pharaoh said, “Did you believe in Him before I have given you permission? This is surely a conspiracy you schemed in the city, in order to expel its people from it. You will surely know.”
t.mubarakpuri: Fir`awn said: "You have believed in him [Musa] before I gave you permission. Surely, this is a plot which you have plotted in the city to drive out its people, but you shall come to know."
t.qarib: Pharaoh said, "How dare you believe in him before I give you permission. This is a plot which you have cunningly devised in [my] town in order to drive out its people. In time you will come to know [my revenge]
t.wahiduddin: Pharaoh said, "You dare believe in Him before I have given you permission? Behold, this is indeed a plot which you have devised in this city in order to drive its people out. But you shall soon know the consequences
t.shakir: Firon said: Do you believe in Him before I have given you permission? Surely this is a plot which you have secretly devised in this city, that you may turn out of it its people, but you shall know
t.haleem: but Pharaoh said, ‘How dare you believe in Him before I have given you permission? This is a plot you have hatched to drive the people out of this city! Soon you will see
t.maariful: Pharaoh said, “You have believed in him before my permission. Undoubtedly, this is a plot you have designed in the city, so that you may expel its people from it. Now you shall know (its end)
t.khattab: Pharaoh threatened, “How dare you believe in him before I give you permission? This must be a conspiracy you devised in the city to drive out its people, but soon you will see
t.ghali: Firaawn (Pharaoh) said, "You have believed in Him before I permit you. Surely this is indeed a scheme you have been scheming in the city that you may drive out its population. Yet you will eventually know
t.irving: Pharaoh said: "You have believed in Him before I permit you to! This is some scheme which you have hatched in the city in order to drive its people out. You will soon find out
t.iwq: Said Pharaoh, "You believed in him before I gave you permission. Indeed, this is a conspiracy which you conspired in the city to expel therefrom its people. But you are going to know

t_id.kemenag: Fir'aun berkata: "Apakah kamu beriman kepadanya sebelum aku memberi izin kepadamu?, sesungguhnya (perbuatan ini) adalah suatu muslihat yang telah kamu rencanakan di dalam kota ini, untuk mengeluarkan penduduknya dari padanya; maka kelak kamu akan mengetahui (akibat perbuatanmu ini);
t_id.sabiq: Melihat itu, Fir'aun pun terkejut dan naik pitam, lalu berkata, "Apakah kalian beriman dan percaya kepada Tuhan Mûsâ dan Hârûn sedangkan aku belum mengizinkan kalian? Sungguh ini merupakan kesepakatan antar kalian dengan Mûsâ dan Hârûn. Ini tidak lain persekongkolan yang kalian lakukan di kota ini (Mesir), agar penduduknya keluar dari kota ini. Maka kalian lihat azab yang akan menimpa kalian, sebagai balasan ketundukan kalian kepada Mûsâ dan Hârûn, serta sebagai siksaan dari persekongkolan dan tipu muslihat yang kalian lakukan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Firaun berkata, "Apakah kamu beriman) lafal aamantum dapat dibaca a amantum (kepadanya) kepada Musa (sebelum aku memberi izin) (kepadamu? Sesungguhnya hal ini) apa yang kamu perbuat ini (adalah suatu muslihat yang telah kamu rencanakan di dalam kota ini untuk mengeluarkan penduduknya daripadanya, maka kelak kamu akan mengetahui) apa yang bakal kamu terima balasannya dariku.
t_id.iwq: Fir'aun berkata: "Apakah kamu beriman kepadanya sebelum aku memberi izin kepadamu?, sesungguhnya (perbuatan ini) adalah suatu muslihat yang telah kamu rencanakan di dalam kota ini, untuk mengeluarkan penduduknya dari padanya; maka kelak kamu akan mengetahui (akibat perbuatanmu ini);

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال فرعون أآمنتم» بتحقيق الهمزتين وإبدال الثانية ألفا «به» بموسى «قبل أن آذن» «لكم إنَّ هذا» الذي صنعتموه «لمكر مكرتموه في المدينة لتخرجوا منها أهلها فسوف تعلمون» ما ينالكم مني
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ فِرۡعَوۡنُ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَمَكۡرٞ مَّكَرۡتُمُوهُ فِي ٱلۡمَدِينَةِ لِتُخۡرِجُواْ مِنۡهَآ أَهۡلَهَاۖ فَسَوۡفَ تَعۡلَمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Firaun berkata, "Apakah kamu beriman) lafal aamantum dapat dibaca a amantum (kepadanya) kepada Musa (sebelum aku memberi izin) (kepadamu? Sesungguhnya hal ini) apa yang kamu perbuat ini (adalah suatu muslihat yang telah kamu rencanakan di dalam kota ini untuk mengeluarkan penduduknya daripadanya, maka kelak kamu akan mengetahui) apa yang bakal kamu terima balasannya dariku

ar: لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَیۡدِیَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَـٰفࣲ ثُمَّ لَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ أَجۡمَعِینَ
ar_simple: لأقطعن أیدیكم وأرجلكم من خلف ثم لأصلبنكم أجمعین

t.sahih-int: I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides; then I will surely crucify you all
t.asad: most certainly shall I cut off your hands and your feet in great numbers, because of [your] perverseness, and then I shall most certainly crucify you, in great numbers, all together
t.yusuf-ali: Be sure I will cut off your hands and your feet on apposite sides, and I will cause you all to die on the cross
t.pickthall: Surely I shall have your hands and feet cut off upon alternate sides. Then I shall crucify you every one
t.arberry: I shall assuredly cut off alternately your hands and feet, then I shall crucify you all together
t.hilali-khan: Surely, I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, then I will crucify you all
t.itani: “I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides; then I will crucify you all.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Surely, I will cut off your hands and your feet from opposite sides, then I will crucify you all."
t.qarib: I will cut off your hands and feet in great numbers because of [your] perverseness, and then I will certainly crucify you together in great numbers
t.wahiduddin: I will cut off your hands and feet on alternate sides and then crucify you all
t.shakir: I will certainly cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, then will I crucify you all together
t.haleem: I will cut off your alternate hands and feet and then crucify you all!’
t.maariful: I will cut off your hands and your legs from opposite sides. Then I will crucify you all together.”
t.khattab: I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you all.”
t.ghali: Indeed I will definitely cut up your hands and your legs alternately, thereafter indeed I will definitely crucify you all together
t.irving: I´ll cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides; then I´ll crucify you all
t.iwq: I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides; then I will surely crucify you all

t_id.kemenag: demi, sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kakimu dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik, kemudian sungguh-sungguh aku akan menyalib kamu semuanya".
t_id.sabiq: Aku bersumpah akan menjadikan kalian sebagai bahan pelajaran. Akan aku potong tangan dan kaki kalian secara bersilang, tangan kanan dengan kaki kiri atau sebaliknya. Lalu, dengan keadaan yang menyeramkan ini, akan kami salib kalian semua. Itu semua dilakukan agar menjadi peringatan bagi siapa yang hendak menipu dan melawan kekuasaan kami."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Demi sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kakimu dengan bersilang secara timbal balik) yakni tangan kanan setiap orang akan dipotong berikut kaki sebelah kirinya (kemudian sungguh-sungguh aku akan menyalibmu semuanya.")
t_id.iwq: demi, sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kakimu dengan bersilang secara bertimbal balik, kemudian sungguh-sungguh aku akan menyalib kamu semuanya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لأقطعنَّ أيديكم وأرجلكم من خلاف» أي يد كل واحد اليمنى ورجله اليسرى «ثم لأصلِّبنَّكم أجمعين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيۡدِيَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَٰفٖ ثُمَّ لَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Demi sesungguhnya aku akan memotong tangan dan kakimu dengan bersilang secara timbal balik) yakni tangan kanan setiap orang akan dipotong berikut kaki sebelah kirinya (kemudian sungguh-sungguh aku akan menyalibmu semuanya)

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ إِنَّاۤ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
ar_simple: قالوا إنا إلى ربنا منقلبون

t.sahih-int: They said, "Indeed, to our Lord we will return
t.asad: They answered: "Verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "For us, We are but sent back unto our Lord
t.pickthall: They said: Lo! We are about to return unto our Lord
t.arberry: They said, 'Surely unto our Lord we are turning
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Verily, we are returning to our Lord
t.itani: They said, “It is to our Lord that we will return.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Verily, we are returning to our Lord."
t.qarib: They answered, "We return to Our Lord
t.wahiduddin: They replied, "We shall surely return to our Lord
t.shakir: They said: Surely to our Lord shall we go back
t.haleem: They said, ‘And so we shall return to our Lord
t.maariful: They said, “We have surely to return to our Lord
t.khattab: They responded, “Surely to our Lord we will ˹all˺ return
t.ghali: They said, "Surely to our Lord we are around (Literally: turning over)
t.irving: They said: "We will be sent home to our Lord
t.iwq: And they said, "Indeed, to our Lord we will return

t_id.kemenag: Ahli-ahli sihir itu menjawab: "Sesungguhnya kepada Tuhanlah kami kembali.
t_id.sabiq: Tetapi ancaman itu tidak membuat mereka bergeming sedikit pun. Keimanan sudah sangat merasuk ke dalam kalbu mereka. Mereka pun berkata kepada Fir'aun, "Sesungguhnya kepada Tuhanlah kami akan kembali dalam naungan rahmat dan kenikmatan ganjaran-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ahli-ahli sihir itu menjawab, "Sesungguhnya kepada Tuhan kamilah) sesudah kami mati dengan cara apa pun (kami kembali) dikembalikan kelak di akhirat.
t_id.iwq: Ahli-ahli sihir itu menjawab: "Sesungguhnya kepada Tuhanlah kami kembali.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا إنا إلى ربِّنا» بعد موتنا بأي وجه كان «منقلبون» راجعون في الآخرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ (مُنقَلِبُونَ: رَاجِعُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ahli-ahli sihir itu menjawab, "Sesungguhnya kepada Tuhan kamilah) sesudah kami mati dengan cara apa pun (kami kembali) dikembalikan kelak di akhirat

ar: وَمَا تَنقِمُ مِنَّاۤ إِلَّاۤ أَنۡ ءَامَنَّا بِءَایَـٰتِ رَبِّنَا لَمَّا جَاۤءَتۡنَاۚ رَبَّنَاۤ أَفۡرِغۡ عَلَیۡنَا صَبۡرࣰا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسۡلِمِینَ
ar_simple: وما تنقم منا إلا أن ءامنا بءایت ربنا لما جاءتنا ربنا أفرغ علینا صبرا وتوفنا مسلمین

t.sahih-int: And you do not resent us except because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour upon us patience and let us die as Muslims [in submission to You]
t.asad: for thou takest vengeance on us only because we have come to believe in our Sustainer's messages as soon as they came to us. O our Sustainer! Shower us with patience in adversity, and make us die as men who have surrendered themselves unto Thee
t.yusuf-ali: But thou dost wreak thy vengeance on us simply because we believed in the Signs of our Lord when they reached us! Our Lord! pour out on us patience and constancy, and take our souls unto thee as Muslims (who bow to thy will)
t.pickthall: Thou takest vengeance on us only forasmuch as we believed the tokens of our Lord when they came unto us. Our Lord! Vouchsafe unto us steadfastness and make us die as men who have surrendered (unto Thee)
t.arberry: Thou takest vengeance upon us only because we have believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour out upon us patience, and gather us unto Thee surrendering
t.hilali-khan: And you take vengeance on us only because we believed in the Ayat (proofs, evidences, lessons, signs, etc.) of our Lord when they reached us! Our Lord! pour out on us patience, and cause us to die as Muslims
t.itani: “You are taking vengeance on us only because we have believed in the signs of our Lord when they have come to us.” “Our Lord! Pour out patience upon us, and receive our souls in submission.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And you take vengeance on us only because we believed in the Ayat of our Lord when they reached us! Our Lord! pour out on us patience, and cause us to die as Muslims."
t.qarib: You take revenge against us only because we have come to believe in our Lord's messages as soon as they came to us. Lord, shower us with patience in hard times and make us die as men who have surrendered themselves to You
t.wahiduddin: You would punish us only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they were shown to us. Our Lord, pour patience upon us, and cause us to die in a state of submission to You
t.shakir: And you do not take revenge on us except because we have believed in the communications of our Lord when they came to us! Our Lord: Pour out upon us patience and cause us to die in submission
t.haleem: Your only grievance against us is that we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour steadfastness upon us and let us die in devotion to You.’
t.maariful: For what fault would you punish us except that we have believed in the signs of our Lord when they appeared to us? O our Lord, pour out patience upon us, and cause us to die as Muslims (those who submit to you.)”
t.khattab: Your rage towards us is only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord! Shower us with perseverance, and let us die while submitting ˹to You˺.”
t.ghali: And in no way do you take vengeance upon us except that we have believed in the signs of our Lord as soon they came to us. Our Lord, pour out upon us patience and take us to Yourself as Muslims
t.irving: You are persecuting us only because we have believed in our Lord´s signs once they were brought us. Our Lord, pour patience over us, and gather us up [at death] as Muslims
t.iwq: And you do not resent us except because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour upon us patience and let us die as Muslims in submission to You

t_id.kemenag: Dan kamu tidak menyalahkan kami, melainkan karena kami telah beriman kepada ayat-ayat Tuhan kami ketika ayat-ayat itu datang kepada kami". (Mereka berdoa): "Ya Tuhan kami, limpahkanlah kesabaran kepada kami dan wafatkanlah kami dalam keadaan berserah diri (kepada-Mu)".
t_id.sabiq: Sebenarnya kamu tidak berbuat ingkar dan menyiksa kami kecuali karena kami telah percaya kepada Mûsâ dan tunduk kepada ayat-ayat Tuhan kami yang jelas dan benar."
